step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture starting from the internal developer requesting an account. The request passes through AWS API Gateway to AWS Step Functions (AVM). This AVM step function reads/writes target state metadata in DynamoDB and triggers AWS Control Tower to provision the AWS account. Once the account is created, a second Step Functions execution (Blueprint) configures the account with required IAM, security, and networking blueprints. Finally, SES sends an email notification back to the user. A separate CodePipeline flow also exists to continuously push blueprint updates directly into vended accounts.

graph.name: Vitesco Technologies Automated Cloud Landing Zone
graph.notes: Automated cloud lending zone allowing internal users to create AWS accounts. Scale: Over 850 accounts created. Platform managed by only four engineers using automated blueprints.

nodes.name: Internal Developer
nodes.notes: Requests new AWS accounts through a self-developed web portal.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Receives the portal metadata and routes requests.

nodes.name: Step Functions (AVM)
nodes.notes: Account Vending Machine orchestrating account provisioning steps.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores target state, CIDR ranges, and account IDs to ensure idempotency.

nodes.name: Control Tower
nodes.notes: Provisions new accounts via a custom Service Catalog item.

nodes.name: Step Functions (Blueprint)
nodes.notes: Deploys customized blueprints (IAM, security, network configurations).

nodes.name: SES Email Service
nodes.notes: Sends details and responsibilities to the account owner.

nodes.name: Vended AWS Account
nodes.notes: The target environment deployed for internal teams.

nodes.name: CodePipeline
nodes.notes: Deploys blueprint changes centrally to customer accounts within 3 hours.

edges.notes: User submits network configurations and metadata.
Saves state and fetches CIDR block ranges.
Launches custom service catalog item to provision account.
Applies IAM, security, and networking configs to the account.
Pushes updated blueprints directly across dev, QA, and prod accounts.
